title: Multi-weight smoke test with worktree isolation and gate simulation
description: Extend the /smoke-test skill with multi-weight support (lightweight + medium), worktree isolation, Option B gate simulation, and concrete test cases per weight class.

in: Weight argument in run-smoke-test.sh ($1 = lightweight|medium, default medium)
Worktree isolation replacing branch checkout (git worktree add)
Concrete test cases: lightweight = create marker file, medium = create utility module with function + config
Option B test_mode in 04b-clarification.md — full gate logic + default response
Option B test_mode in 07-plan-approval.md — full gate logic + auto-approve
SKILL.md updated to pass weight argument
out: Custom test framework or assertion library
Changes to the completion gate (already has test_mode from iteration 1)

dod.criterion: Running /smoke-test lightweight creates an isolated worktree, runs a lightweight directive that creates a marker file, and reports pass/fail per step
Running /smoke-test medium creates an isolated worktree, runs a medium directive that creates a utility module, and reports pass/fail per step
Clarification gate (04b) runs full synthesis logic in test_mode then auto-responds
Approve gate (07) runs full plan presentation in test_mode then auto-approves
Worktree and test directive artifacts are cleaned up after test completes (pass or fail)

tasks.title: Replace branch checkout with worktree isolation in run-smoke-test.sh
tasks.user_scenario: The smoke test creates an isolated worktree so it never conflicts with the user's current branch or other running sessions.
tasks.description: Replace the `git checkout -b` approach (lines 269-274) with `git worktree add`. Update the cleanup trap (lines 77-117) to use `git worktree remove` and delete the worktree directory. Remove the ORIGINAL_BRANCH tracking since the user's checkout is never changed. Update the agent spawn (lines 280-298) to pass the worktree path as the working directory.

dod.criterion: Script uses `git worktree add <path> -b <branch>` instead of `git checkout -b`; no `git checkout` calls remain except in error handling
Cleanup trap removes worktree with `git worktree remove` and deletes the branch; user's current branch is never changed
Agent spawn command includes the worktree path as the working directory so the /directive session operates in the isolated copy
Running the script while on any branch does not change HEAD or leave dangling worktrees on success or failure (trap handles both)

tasks.title: Accept weight argument and implement per-weight skip sets and test directives
tasks.user_scenario: The CEO runs `/smoke-test medium` or `/smoke-test lightweight` and sees the correct skip set applied and the correct weight in the test directive.
tasks.description: Accept $1 as the weight argument (default: medium, valid: lightweight|medium). Replace the hardcoded `is_medium_skip` function with a weight-aware skip set. Update the test directive.json template to use the passed weight. Update SKILL.md to document both weight classes and pass $ARGUMENTS to the script as $1.

dod.criterion: Script accepts $1 as weight (lightweight|medium), defaults to medium, rejects invalid values with a clear error message
Skip set for lightweight: brainstorm is skipped; skip set for medium: brainstorm is skipped; both auto-approve clarification and approve gates
Test directive.json `weight` field matches the passed argument (not hardcoded to medium)
SKILL.md documents both weight classes and passes the weight argument from $ARGUMENTS to the script
Results table header shows the actual weight class used (e.g., 'Weight: lightweight')

tasks.title: Add Option B test_mode gate simulation to clarification and plan-approval pipeline docs
tasks.user_scenario: When the pipeline runs in test_mode, the clarification and plan-approval steps execute their full logic but auto-respond instead of waiting for CEO input, so the smoke test validates real pipeline behavior.
tasks.description: In 04b-clarification.md: add test_mode check at top of Step 2 that runs full synthesis then auto-approves. In 07-plan-approval.md: add test_mode check that runs full plan presentation then auto-approves. Completion gate already has test_mode — no changes needed.

dod.criterion: 04b-clarification.md has a test_mode check that runs full intent synthesis (Step 1) then auto-approves at Step 2 with log line '[TEST_MODE] Auto-approved clarification'
07-plan-approval.md has a test_mode check that runs full complexity floor check and plan construction then auto-approves with log line '[TEST_MODE] Auto-approved plan'
test_mode gate simulation is additive (inserted as a conditional before CEO interaction), not a replacement of existing lightweight/medium auto-approve logic
11-completion-gate.md is NOT modified (already has test_mode handling)

tasks.title: Create lightweight-specific test directive content and validate end-to-end
tasks.user_scenario: When the CEO runs `/smoke-test lightweight`, the test directive is appropriately scoped for a lightweight pipeline run and the results show skipped steps clearly.
tasks.description: Update directive.md and directive.json templates to be weight-appropriate. Ensure results table distinguishes 'skipped' from 'auto-approved' with clear evidence strings. Verify lightweight pipeline completes without false timeouts.

dod.criterion: Lightweight test directive.json template has weight='lightweight' and appropriate initial state
Results table distinguishes 'skipped' steps from 'auto-approved' steps with clear evidence strings showing the weight-based reason
Script handles lightweight pipeline completing faster than medium without false timeout or missed step transitions
Running `bash run-smoke-test.sh lightweight` produces a valid results table with correct skip/auto-approve annotations
